title: Forced alignment / phoneme timing for learning tools
context: Language learners and TTS/accessibility developers benefit from phoneme-level timing in audio: knowing where each phoneme segment starts and ends within a recording enables interactive learning tools (highlight-as-you-listen) and improves TTS training data quality. Forced alignment matches the IPA transcription to the audio waveform and produces a phoneme timing file. This is assistive, non-prescriptive, and the output must be human-confirmed before inclusion. The task is noted in TASKS.md as 'Assistive; human-confirmed'. This is a backlog item, unscheduled.
objective: Implement a forced-alignment pipeline that takes a Recording and its IPA transcription and produces a phoneme-timing file (start/end timestamps per phoneme segment), with a human-review step before the timing file is committed to the corpus.
acceptanceCriteria: Forced alignment pipeline takes a Recording (audio file) and its IPA transcription as inputs and produces a phoneme-timing file (JSON with start/end timestamps per phoneme).
The pipeline uses an open-licensed forced-aligner (no proprietary models).
Every generated timing file is marked as requiring human review; a reviewer must confirm the alignment before it is committed to the corpus.
The alignment output format is documented and validates against a JSON Schema.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass.
Maintainer reviews and merges the PR.
output: Forced-alignment pipeline package (TypeScript/ESM, wrapping an open-licensed aligner) committed via PR with human-review gate and passing CI.
